title: You Can Actually Think
description: Not about lost jobs or AI generated slop. The thing people are missing is the weight of the word intelligence, and the fact that they can actually think. AI takes your jobs because your beautiful mind was never supposed to be doing that.

title: Offloading and the Compression of Knowledge
description: Evolution shrank our guts once we offloaded digestion to fire, and it may be shrinking our brains as we offload cognition to silicon. On compressing the knowledge we gather, and proof writing becoming a hobby.

title: Life Doesn't Operate in Extremes
description: Why Tegmark's Life 3.0 feels too black and white: perfecting an AGI on its own terms converges on perfecting an organism, and other minds appreciating the universe would be no defeat at all.

title: Quantized Mind
description: Mathematics does not flow, it jumps, and self-reference is always the ceiling that forces the jump. On the mind as a system mapping its own levels from inside.

title: By Design
description: A half-formed theory that the laws are built to keep us pointed at the purpose, written mindlessly to climb out of a first research block.

title: The Dvole Cycle
description: A theory of intelligence, clutter, and offloading: why ever more capable AI is not a threat to purpose but intelligence decluttering itself, and what survives every transition.

title: Borderline Society and Existentials
description: On reading a lot lately, the project I won't name, and why understanding the universe might be the one purpose that has never changed.
